video_title: 10 Craziest #DDD Lasagna Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Marsigliano's Pizzeria and More
restaurants.guy_intro: So Hunter and I are right off the strip in Las Vegas. And Hunter, when you were going to UNLV, what was one of your favorite places to eat at? ... This is Marsigliano's Pizzeria and More.

dishes.description: A unique pizza combining traditional lasagna and pizza, featuring bolognese sauce, marinara, mozzarella, ricotta, and a second crust on top, finished with garlic butter and parmesan.
dishes.guy_response: Oh my gosh. It's got great texture. It's got great chew. I think it's a genius. I see it. Huh? You're a madman. Man's an animal. Pizza lasagna ready to go. Top it off. The top layer is kind of crispy with that garlicy flavor. Right? Pizza and lasagna in the same dish? It's amazing.

restaurants.name: Rosie's
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Monterey, California, known for Cannery Row and awesome seafood. Well, I'm in the locals area. I'm downtown. And check out a joint that the locals love, an old school, family-run Italian joint. This is Rosie's.

dishes.description: Lasagna with a special filling of browned ground beef, onions, mushrooms, oregano, garlic, basil, salt, pepper, nutmeg, and spinach, layered with red sauce, pasta sheets, ricotta, parmesan, and jack cheese.
dishes.guy_response: This has got multiple layers, super rich and thick, tender pasta, creamy, all the balance. That's the example of lasagna like your mom used to make. Jimbo, nice job, ma'am. Nice job. Mom did it right. She sure did.

restaurants.name: Cafe Nona
restaurants.guy_intro: So about two miles west of downtown Nashville on Murphy Road, to check out a joint where a chef who's a transplant from New York City has had a couple places. You've probably heard of them, the dancing bear, the red rooster, and for the last 15 years, he's been rocking this place. This is Cafe Nona.

dishes.description: Eight-layer lasagna featuring marinara sauce, pasta sheets, a ricotta mixture with cream, shallots, garlic, sage, oregano, basil, and crushed red pepper, spinach, and butternut squash. Finished with Mornay sauce, Swiss chard, marinara, Parmesan, basil, and fried julienne vegetables.
dishes.guy_response: And this is the kind of lasagna that your grandmother would make when you were a kid. This is sort of my version. I wanted to put a little spin on it. You know what I like is look at the amount of spinach this guy puts in his lasagna. Nice, big, thick noodles in there. You definitely don't hold back on the cheese. Three different sauces in there. I mean, you put a lot of time and work into it. Yes, sir. And it tastes like it. Nice job, chef. Thank you. Really nice.

restaurants.name: Mulberry Italian Ristorante
restaurants.guy_intro: Welcome to Mulberry Italian Ristorante. Lasagna for table seven. This place is awesome. I love it. All right. A lot of Italian food in Buffalo. How does this rate? It's the best. Yolky Mulberry, the food beyond belief. It's just wonderful. Like eating at home, sailing at home. No surprise there.

dishes.description: A massive 45-pound lasagna made with handmade double zero flour pasta, homemade tomato sauce, sliced meatballs, mozzarella, parmesan, chicken, stuffed hot pepper mixture, sausage and peppers, and ricotta with parsley and garlic.
dishes.guy_response: Wow, man. Good? That's not good. That's great. It's rich. It's flavorful. Everything you want a lasagna to be. And quality. Mm. My man. Nice job, Joe. We have to get you your tattoo. That's some gangster lasagna right there. I don't whip that out all the time. That lasagna, that deserves it. Just a lot of meat. It's nice and spicy. It's got the hot peppers in there. Did you really eat that whole mountain? I'm trying to finish the rest. I'm waving the flag, man. I'm done. Awesome food. I would come here and munch every single day, then I'd go.

restaurants.name: El Fogon de la 31
restaurants.guy_intro: All right, Chef Antonia, last stop. What do you think? Vegan Puerto Rican food blew my mind. I do not know if I have any more room for this. Well, you've got to have room because this place in particular is pretty amazing. This is where the locals go. Matter of fact, we're an industrial area. This place is only open Monday through Friday. But everybody that loves that true Puerto Rican food comes here. I do not know how you do this every day. This is el fogon de la 31.

dishes.description: Puerto Rican lasagna made with layers of fried yellow plantains, egg as a binder, picadillo (ground beef, sofrito, vinegar, spices), and cheese. Served with arroz y habichuelas (rice and beans).
dishes.guy_response: I'm going lasagna. The beans are so unbelievable. I don't know that I've ever been this excited about beans. Silky, and they just melt in your mouth. The sweet plantain. I fried it really well to where it's got great texture. Never in a million years did I think, I mean, honestly, as an Italian, that I'm going to eat lasagna with rice and beans. And the sauce on the beans, as you mix that with the picadillo, it's almost like the sauce that was needed for the lasagna. Really good. 10 of 10. The combination of the flavor of the meat and the sweet is very good.

restaurants.name: Matteo's Osteria
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Maui, just up the street from the Ondas Hotel. You boys found it. Good deal. Dad, we've been here before. Mom, we sat in the back. We came around the back, actually. I knew this place sounded familiar. It's a husband and wife team. She does the baking. He scratch makes everything. And I'm telling you, everyone talks about it. This is Matteo's Osteria.

dishes.description: Lasagna with homemade bolognese (Italian sausage, mirepoix, ground beef, pork mortadella, Sangiovese wine, rosemary, imported tomato sauce, veal demi), homemade pasta, bechamel sauce, Parmigiano-Reggiano, and mozzarella, baked in a terracotta bowl.
dishes.guy_response: It's delicious. This doesn't hurt us at all. This is rustic, super balanced. You really get the bolognese. It's not heavy. But it's bright, really clean flavors. We should have popped a couple in for the boys. My mouth just sounds, it's, uh. I got a full tray for them. Your lasagna? Yeah. The toasted, melted cheese really gives it a nice little crunch. It's good stuff. I think I'll be able to actually finish the entire dish. And that's not common with lasagna. But this, I could pound it out.

restaurants.name: Grammy's Goodies
restaurants.guy_intro: I heard about this family that started off in the fair business, and then they went and opened a restaurant. This is Grammy's Goodies. Coming to this place is like going to your grandma's house. Because Grammy's is actually filled with grandma's recipes, which Vicki Corder, her husband Jeff, and their kids first started selling at festivals. And when business boomed, they expanded to a full-fledged restaurant.

dishes.description: Lasagna featuring 'Grammy's' sundae sauce (pork ribs, net bones, garlic, onion, parsley, crushed tomatoes, basil, tomato paste), 'Nana's' meatballs (pork, ground beef, onions, bread crumbs, basil, garlic, parsley, Romano cheese), a ricotta cheese mixture (ricotta, black pepper, eggs, parsley, mozzarella, provolone, Romano cheese), sausage, and noodles.
dishes.guy_response: So I know that this is the lasagna portion for the entire family. Lots of ricotta, delicious meatballs, tender pasta, dynamite sauce. You make this roll yourself also? Yes. Well, I'm going to tell you what. This is old school, American Italian, exactly the kind of lasagna I would expect to go and get at Grammy's house. Dynamite.
